criteria: The agent has read Section 3 completely to understand the AVS-QA dataset construction pipeline, including the Actor–Evaluator–Critic three-stage process.
The agent has read Section 4 completely to understand the RAVEN framework, including the modality-specific encoders, projection layers, QuART module, and training objective.
The agent has read Section 5 completely to understand the three-stage training pipeline: modality-text pre-training, query-token alignment joint-training, and modal-discrepancy-aware fine-tuning.

The agent has read Appendix C to understand the architecture details of the LIMU-BERT sensor encoder, SigLIP video encoder pre-training procedure, and the projection layer designs (2-layer MLP for audio, STC connector for video).

The agent has read Appendix E to understand the training hyperparameters, including token counts (L_a=1496, L_v=1352, L_s=120), embedding dimension E=3584, 6-layer transformer with 8 heads, LoRA rank, optimizer (AdamW), and dataset used at each training stage.
The agent has read Appendix D to understand the cross-modal mismatch generation algorithm (Algorithm 1), including noise injection, temporal reversal, sensor perturbation, and modal replacement.
The plan includes implementing the three-stage Actor–Evaluator–Critic pipeline for AVS-QA dataset curation.
The plan includes loading and integrating BLIP-2 for frame captioning, YOLOv11 for object detection and localization, and Qwen2-Audio-7B for audio transcription and event labeling in the Actor stage.
The plan includes extracting IMU statistical features (mean, RMS, skewness) over 15-second windows at 200 Hz for the sensor modality in the Actor stage.
The plan includes the Evaluator stage for modality-consistency filtering, verifying that each QA pair is grounded in its referenced modality.

The plan includes the Critic stage: five LLM critics (LLaVA-1.5, Gemini Pro, Qwen-VL, GPT-4o, LLaMA-3) scoring QA pairs across five axes: answerability, hallucination robustness, cross-modal grounding, specificity, and relevance.
The plan includes implementing the RAVEN architecture with SigLIP-so-400m as video encoder, BEATs as audio encoder, and LIMU-BERT as sensor encoder.
The plan includes implementing QuART with multi-head attention between query embedding and concatenated multi-modal token matrix, followed by a learnable relevance projection head W^R.

The plan includes the three-stage training pipeline: Stage I (freeze encoders, train projections), Stage II (freeze encoders and projections, train QuART and LoRA-adapted decoder), and Stage III (add perturbations and entropy regularization).

The plan includes implementing the cross-modal mismatch generation algorithm (Algorithm 1) with stochastic perturbations (noise injection, temporal reversal, sensor jitter, modal replacement) for Stage III fine-tuning.
The plan includes running evaluation on seven QA benchmarks: AVSD, MUSIC-QA, AVSSD, MSVD-QA, MSRVTT-QA, ActivityNet-QA, and EgoThink.

The Actor stage of AVS-QA is implemented: given a synchronized (video, audio, sensor) triplet, meta-information is extracted using BLIP-2 (frame captioning), YOLOv11 (object detection), Qwen2-Audio-7B (audio), and a statistical IMU extractor (mean, RMS, skewness over 15-second windows at 200 Hz), and concatenated into a natural-language prompt.
The Actor stage generates four QA types (open-ended, closed-ended, multiple-choice, true/false) using the enriched prompt, with five candidate answers generated for open-ended questions.

The Evaluator stage is implemented: each candidate QA pair is verified for modality grounding (e.g., motion questions require sensor variance spikes, visual/audio references must align with detected objects or acoustic summaries); QA pairs failing grounding are discarded, and diversity of single- vs. cross-modality types is enforced.

The Critic stage is implemented using an ensemble of five instruction-tuned LLMs (LLaVA-1.5, Gemini Pro, Qwen-VL, GPT-4o, LLaMA-3) to score QA pairs on five axes (answerability, hallucination robustness, cross-modal grounding, specificity, relevance) on a 5-point Likert scale; QA pairs where 2 or more axes score <3 from at least 3 of 5 LLMs are discarded.
The AVS-QA dataset curation pipeline is complete: starting from ~387K initial QA pairs, filtering results in ~300K final high-quality QA pairs drawn from Ego4D and EPIC-Kitchens-100 clips.

The video encoder (SigLIP-so-400m) is integrated: video frames are uniformly sampled, encoded to obtain z_v in R^{L_v x E} (L_v=1352, E=3584), and connected via the STC (spatio-temporal convolutional) connector using RegStage with 3D convolution for downsampling.

The audio encoder (BEATs) is integrated: audio is converted to a Kaldi-fbank spectrogram and encoded to obtain z_a in R^{L_a x E} (L_a=1496, E=3584), with a two-layer MLP projection head aligning output to the LLM dimension.

The sensor encoder (LIMU-BERT) is integrated: multi-axis IMU streams are encoded to obtain z_s in R^{L_s x E} (L_s=120, E=3584). LIMU-BERT uses a normalization and sensor fusion layer, a transformer encoder with cross-layer parameter sharing, and a span-masking MLM pretraining objective.
The language decoder (Qwen2-7B-Instruct) is integrated and its tokenizer maps query Q to token embeddings z_q in R^{L_q x E}.

The QuART module is implemented: visual, audio, and sensor token sequences (z_v, z_a, z_s) are concatenated into a unified token matrix Z in R^{L x E} where L = L_v + L_a + L_s; sinusoidal positional embeddings are applied to preserve temporal order.
The QuART multi-head attention is implemented: Q = z_q * W^Q, K = Z * W^K, V = Z * W^V with W^Q, W^K, W^V in R^{E x d_k} (d_k=448, 8 heads), and M = softmax(QK^T / sqrt(d_k)) * V.

The QuART relevance projection head W^R in R^{E x L} is implemented to compute query-conditioned token relevance scores: alpha = softmax(M * W^R), and the fused context vector C = sum_j(alpha_j * Z_j) is computed over all L tokens.

The training objective is implemented: L_RAVEN = L_QuART + lambda * L_reg, where L_QuART is autoregressive cross-entropy over the decoder output conditioned on C, and L_reg = sum_j(alpha_j * log(alpha_j)) is the entropy-based regularizer promoting sparse token selection.

Stage I (Modality-Text Pre-Training) is implemented: all three pretrained encoders (Phi^v, Phi^a, Phi^s) and the LLM decoder Pi are frozen; only the corresponding projection heads P^m are updated sequentially on modality-text pairs (Vision-Text: 12.2M pairs, Audio-Text: 400K WavCaps, Sensor-Text: 205K OpenSQA+SensorCaps).

Stage II (Query-Token Alignment Joint-Training) is implemented: all modality encoders and projection heads are frozen; QuART is initialized from scratch and trained; the LLM decoder Pi is fine-tuned using LoRA with rank 256 on the AVS-QA dataset (403K pairs, combined with AVQA, AVSSD, MUSIC-AVQA, AVSD); lambda=0 (no regularization); L_QuART is conditioned on context embeddings C.

Stage III (Modal-Discrepancy Aware Fine-Tuning) is implemented: frozen encoders process perturbed inputs D'={v', a', s'} generated by Algorithm 1; entropy regularization is activated with lambda=0.001; 510K pairs from AVQA, AVSSD, MUSIC-AVQA, AVSD, and AVS-QA are used.

The cross-modal mismatch generation algorithm (Algorithm 1) is implemented: for each modality independently with probability 0.5, audio is perturbed by ADDNOISE/REVERSE/REPLACEWITHIRRELEVANT/NOPERTURBATION; video by ADDNOISE/REVERSE/REPLACEWITHIRRELEVANT/NOPERTURBATION; sensor by ADDJITTER/REPLACEWITHIRRELEVANT/NOPERTURBATION.
The LoRA adapter is implemented with rank r=256, applied to the Qwen2-7B-Instruct decoder during Stage II and III training.

The training configuration matches the paper: AdamW optimizer, learning rate 1e-3 for projection layers and 1e-5 for encoder fine-tuning, weight decay 0.03, local batch size 1, global batch size 4, 6-layer QuART transformer with 8 heads, d_k=448.

The GPT-3.5-turbo-based evaluation metric is implemented following Maaz et al. (2023): the evaluator is prompted with question, correct answer, and predicted answer to return binary_pred ('yes'/'no'), coherence (float 0–1), and score (float 0–1).
The full RAVEN model (encoders + projection layers + QuART + LoRA-adapted Qwen2-7B-Instruct) with 8.5B total parameters has been trained through all three stages without error.

The AVS-QA dataset curation pipeline has been executed successfully to produce at least 300K QA pairs covering four question types (open-ended, closed-ended, multiple-choice, true/false) across egocentric video clips from Ego4D and EPIC-Kitchens-100.
Stage I training has been executed successfully: projection heads for all three modalities are trained on their respective modality-text datasets (vision 12.2M, audio 400K, sensor 205K).
Stage II training has been executed successfully: QuART module and LoRA-adapted decoder are jointly trained on AVS-QA and auxiliary QA datasets (403K pairs).
Stage III training has been executed successfully: the model is fine-tuned on perturbed multi-modal inputs with entropy regularization (lambda=0.001) using 510K pairs.
Evaluation has been executed on the six exocentric QA benchmarks (AVSD, MUSIC-QA, AVSSD, MSVD-QA, MSRVTT-QA, ActivityNet-QA) and on EgoThink (egocentric).
Evaluation on AVS-QA held-out test set (58K samples) has been executed with ablations across modality combinations (V only, V+A, V+A+S) reporting Completeness, Coherence, Accuracy, and Avg metrics.
Cross-modal mismatch evaluation has been executed by running RAVEN_{I,II} and RAVEN_{I-III} on perturbed inputs across AVSD, MUSIC-QA, MSVD-QA, ActivityNet-QA, and AVS-QA.

Ablation experiments on training stages and loss conditioning have been executed: Stage II only (L_QuART|Z vs L_QuART|C), and Stage III with varying lambda values (no reg, 1, 0.1, 0.01, 0.001) across AVSD, MUSIC-QA, AVSSD, MSVD-QA, ActivityNet-QA, and AVS-QA.
Ablation experiments comparing QuART against raw attention, Q-Former, and HierarQ have been executed, reporting accuracy on AVSD, MUSIC-QA, MSVD-QA, ActivityNet-QA, and MSRVTT-QA.
The execution outcomes match Table 2: RAVEN achieves AVSD 55.1 (+3.6%), MUSIC-QA 69.8 (+5.0%), MSVD-QA 73.3 (+4.2%), MSRVTT-QA 63.1 (+5.4%), ActivityNet-QA 57.6 (+8.0%) over the best baselines.
The execution outcomes match Table 3: RAVEN achieves EgoThink average 0.54 (+14.8%) and AVS-QA average 0.67 (+7.5%) with Completeness 0.71 and Coherence 0.69.
The execution outcomes match Table 4: RAVEN with V+A+S achieves Completeness 0.78, Coherence 0.82, Accuracy 0.73, Avg 0.78 on AVS-QA, with the sensor modality providing an additional +16.4% over V+A.

The execution outcomes match Table 5: under cross-modal mismatch, RAVEN_{I-III} achieves AVSD 54.9, MUSIC-QA 69.2, MSVD-QA 72.8, ActivityNet-QA 57.2, and AVS-QA Avg 0.75, consistently outperforming all baselines by up to 50.23%.

The execution outcomes match Table 6: the best performance (AVSD 55.1, MUSIC-QA 69.8, AVS-QA Avg 0.78) is achieved with Stage III, L_QuART|C conditioning, and lambda=0.001; conditioning on Z vs C in Stage II yields 0.44 vs 0.49 on AVS-QA Avg; lambda=1.0 degrades AVS-QA Avg to 0.30.

The execution outcomes match Table 7: QuART with 45M parameters outperforms Q-Former (188M) and HierarQ (390M) on all benchmarks, achieving AVSD 55.1, MUSIC-QA 69.8, MSVD-QA 73.3, ActivityNet-QA 57.6, MSRVTT-QA 63.1.
The execution outcomes match Tables 10 and 11: SigLIP achieves MSVD-QA 73.3 vs ViT-H/14 67.5, and BEATs achieves MSVD-QA 73.3 vs Whisper-Small 68.1, confirming encoder choice impact.

The execution outcomes match Table 12: uniform frame sampling achieves AVSD 55.1, MUSIC-QA 69.8, comparable to oracle (55.2/70.1) and fixed stride (54.9/69.3), while random sampling degrades performance (45.2/55.6).
The execution outcomes match Figure 14: LoRA rank ablation shows performance peaks at r=256, with AVSD ~55 and ActivityNet-QA ~58, while lower ranks (50, 100) show monotonically lower performance.
The execution outcomes match Table 13: QuART (AVSSD 70.2, MSRVTT-QA 63.1) outperforms general fusion approaches ImageBind (27.8/27.8), MBT (64.1/-), and AVFIC (-/19.4).
